description: Vaara adversarial corpus v1 — structured tool-call attacks for scorer evaluation.

prompt_injection.description: Attacks where adversarial content in a tool output or user input manipulates the agent into performing an unintended action.

jailbreak.description: Attempts to bypass agent policy via roleplay, hypothetical framing, or alignment override patterns (DAN, developer-mode, etc.).

tool_misuse.description: Using legitimate tools in destructive or boundary-violating ways (rm -rf /, mass DB drops, bulk email to non-opted-in recipients).

credential_exfil.description: Attempts to extract, transmit, or exfiltrate API keys, passwords, tokens, or private keys.

privilege_escalation.description: Attempts to gain capabilities beyond the agent's intended scope (sudo, role escalation, disabling safeguards, modifying own permissions).

data_exfil.description: Extracting private user/company data via tool calls that look benign but have exfiltration intent (screenshots to external, emails containing customer DB dumps).

destructive_actions.description: Intentional data loss or irreversible destructive operations (DROP TABLE, wipe disk, delete backups, irreversible git operations on shared branches).

ssrf_via_tools.description: Using the agent's network-capable tools for internal network reconnaissance, cloud metadata exfil (169.254.169.254), or port scanning.
